LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT MERCEDES HAS ESTABLISHED THAT IT CANNOT BE LIABLE FOR EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES BECAUSE IT WAS NOT THE MANUFACTURER OF THE CAR. MERCEDES SHIFTS THE BURDEN ON THIS ISSUE BASED ON THE DINGMAN DECLARATION. IN OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT MERCEDES WAS NOT THE MANUFACTURER. (UMF 1). PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT 7 ONLY ALLEGES THAT MERCEDES WAS THE MANUFACTURER - NOT THE DISTRIBUTOR-AND PLAINTIFF CANNOT AVOID SUMMARY JUDGMENT BY RAISING UNPLEADED THEORIES OF LIABILITY. FURTHER, THE DINGMAN DEPOSITION ESTABLISHES THAT MERCEDES IS NOT THE DISTRIBUTOR. AT MOST, PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT THE MERCEDES WEBSITE IS NOT ENTIRELY ACCURATE. IN ANY EVENT, EXHIBIT J TO THE JOHNSON DECLARATION (THE PAGE FROM THE MERCEDES WEBSITE) IS FROM JANUARY 17, 2012, WHICH IS WELL AFTER THE RELEVANT DATES IN THIS CASE.ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY PLAINTIFF COOK, JASON

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JASON COOK'S MOTION TO STRIKE OR TAX DAIMLER COSTS IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED ONLY AS TO $73.82 IN TRAVEL COSTS WHICH DAIMLER VEHICLE INNOVATIONS USA LLC ("DVI") CONCEDES ARE AN ARITHMETIC ERROR. DEFENDANT DVI IS A PREVAILING PARTY UNDER CCP 1332(A)(4) BECAUSE IT IS A "DEFENDANT AS AGAINST THOSE PLAINTIFFS WHO DO NOT RECOVERY ANY RELIEF AGAINST THAT DEFENDANT." THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANT DVI'S CONTESTED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. JUDGMENT WAS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANT AND AGAINST PLAINTIFF JASON COOK. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE COSTS WERE NOT REASONABLE OR NECESSARY. COSTS ARE AWARDED TO DEFENDANT DVI IN THE AMOUNT OF $1841.28.ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

DEFENDANT MB IS A PREVAILING PARTY UNDER CCP 1332(A)(4) BECAUSE IT IS A "DEFENDANT AS AGAINST THOSE PLAINTIFFS WHO DO NOT RECOVERY ANY RELIEF AGAINST THAT DEFENDANT." LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAIMLER VEHICLE INNOVATIONS USA LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANT DAIMLER FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN ON ISSUE 1 (CAUSE OF ACTION PER CC 1793.2(D)(2) FAILS) BECAUSE IT FAILED TO PROVIDE A COPY OF THE WRITTEN WARRANTY WITH ITS MOVING PAPERS. THE WARRANTY WAS NOT ATTACHED TO THE GOLD DECLARATION, ALTHOUGH REFERRED TO. THE COURT HAS NOT HAD THE OPPORTUNITY TO REVIEW THE EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN ON ISSUE 2 (IMPLIED WARRANTY) BASED ON MEXIA V. RINKER BOAT CO. INC. (2009) 174 CAL.APP.4TH 1297. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN ON ISSUES 3 AND 4 (REMEDIES) BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE CLAIMS CANNOT BE ESTABLISHED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Mtn To Strike Or Tax Mercedez Costs
Off Calendar Apr-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARK A. JOHNSON, ESQ., 619.374.4100, FOR PLANTIFF JASON COOK.
JOSEPH R. WHEELER, ESQ., 916.780.4050, FOR DEFENDANTS DAIMLER VEHICLE INNOVATIONS, USA AND MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF JASON COOK'S Mtn To Strike Or Tax Mercedez Costs is denied. The motion to tax costs is granted only as to $73.82 in travel costs which Mercedes-Benz USA LLC ("MB") concedes are an arithmetic error. Defendant MB is a prevailing party under CCP 1332(a)(4) because it is a "defendant as against those plaintiffs who do not recovery any relief against that defendant." The Court granted Defendant MB's contested motion for summary judgment. Judgment was entered in favor of Defendant and against Plaintiff Jason Cook. Plaintiff has failed to show that the costs were not reasonable or necessary. Costs are awarded to Defendant MB in the amount of $1841.28. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Mtn To Strike Or Tax Daimler Costs
Off Calendar Apr-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARK A. JOHNSON, ESQ., 619.374.4100, FOR PLANTIFF JASON COOK.
JOSEPH R. WHEELER, ESQ., 916.780.4050, FOR DEFENDANTS DAIMLER VEHICLE INNOVATIONS, USA AND MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF JASON COOK'S MOTION To Strike Or Tax Daimler Costs is denied. The motion to tax costs is granted only as to $73.82 in travel costs which Daimler Vehicle Innovations USA LLC ("DVI") concedes are an arithmetic error. Defendant DVI is a prevailing party under CCP 1332(a)(4) because it is a "defendant as against those plaintiffs who do not recovery any relief against that defendant." The Court granted Defendant DVI's contested motion for summary judgment. Judgment was entered in favor of Defendant and against Plaintiff Jason Cook. Plaintiff has failed to show that the costs were not reasonable or necessary. Costs are awarded to Defendant DVI in the amount of $1841.28.Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Johnson, Esq., 619.374.4100, for plaintiff BluPlanet, LLC.
Joseph Wheeler, Esq., 916.780.4050, for defendant Daimler Vehicle Innovations USA LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT DAIMLER VEHICLE INNOVATIONS USA LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied in its entirety. Defendant Daimler fails to shift the burden on issue 1 (cause of action per CC 1793.2(d)(2) fails) because it failed to provide a copy of the written warranty with its moving papers. The warranty was not attached to the Gold declaration, although referred to. The Court has not had the opportunity to review the evidence. Defendant fails to shift the burden on issue 2 (implied warranty) based on Mexia v. Rinker Boat Co. Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1297. Defendant fails to shift the burden on issues 3 and 4 (remedies) because defendant has not shown that the claims cannot be established. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Johnson, Esq., 619.374.4100, for plaintiff BluPlanet, LLC; No other appearances.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant Mercedes has established that it cannot be liable for express or implied warranties because it was not the manufacturer of the car. Mercedes shifts the burden on this issue based on the Dingman Declaration. In opposition, Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff does not dispute that Mercedes was not the manufacturer. (UMF 1). Plaintiff's Amended Complaint 7 only alleges that Mercedes was the manufacturer - not the distributor-and Plaintiff cannot avoid summary judgment by raising unpleaded theories of liability. Further, the Dingman deposition establishes that Mercedes is not the distributor. At most, Plaintiff has shown that the Mercedes website is not entirely accurate. In any event, Exhibit J to the Johnson Declaration (the page from the Mercedes website) is from January 17, 2012, which is well after the relevant dates in this case.Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
